core_loop: This lane is process/infra, not gameplay: it governs how design decisions (combat R2/R3, contest rebuild stages, mass-battle, propagation-spec) get authored, ratified, and sequenced into the corpus that eventually drives play. Its "loop" is Jordan reviews/merges → ratifies-by-default (ED-1094) → steering docs should update → next session bootstraps from them. That loop is currently broken at the last link.

north_star.collision_feed: This territory feeds the collision engine only indirectly: it determines whether the architecture (propagation-spec, holonic doctrine) that should carry cross-scale consequence into play actually gets authored coherently and stays legible to the next session. Right now it feeds inconsistent status signals into downstream work.
north_star.verdict: Points at the North Star in intent (propagation-spec explicitly targets cross-scale consequence) but the sequencing layer itself has drifted enough that a session bootstrapping from workplan v5 or roadmap_state.yaml alone would build on stale premises -- a meta-level legibility failure that risks propagating into gameplay decisions.

nq.observation: Propagation-spec (ED-1093, CANONICAL) is the doc explicitly named to carry the 'cross-scale consequence' Omega-clause, but zero mentions of threadwork/Coherence appear in it.
Sim's ~87%/0%/0% degenerate win-share has no regression oracle beyond a smoke test, unaddressed through a month that hardened CI everywhere else.
Spear/polearm dominance measurably improved by R2 (94-96%->78-93%) but the declared 55-75% contested band is still not met.

threadwork_junctures.evidence: 0 grep hits for 'threadwork'/'Coherence' in the doc despite it being pitched as the cross-scale consequence spine
same grep, 0 hits; doctrine cross-maps container/wrapper vocab onto Key-substrate/sim-ladder but not thread state
ED-911 (open, P1) already tracks 'no threadwork interface'; R2/R3 scope (closing-distance, weapon-model) did not add one
grep confirms Coherence/threadwork language retained through the contest rebuild

legibility.note: This is a process/architecture territory, not a scene; there is no single actor/season state to read a dramatic-legibility answer from. The closer analogue -- 'can a designer tell what's actually ratified vs. open right now' -- fails: workplan v5 and decision_queue disagree on J-38's status.

degenerate_candidates: sim/mc_v18 seeded win-share ~87%/0%/0%, no regression oracle -- decision_queue.md item 8 / CLAUDE.md §7
spear/yari/guisarme/poleaxe post-R2 still 75-93% vs declared 55-75% band -- i8_capstone_audit.md, R3 (PR#76) assigned to close it

directions.top-down: P-14/Omega cross-scale-consequence philosophy is not engaged by name in the month's two flagship architecture docs (propagation-spec, holonic doctrine).
directions.bottom-up: sim/mc_v18's degenerate ~87% win-share persists untouched while every adjacent CI/tooling layer got hardened this month.
directions.lateral: contest-rebuild sequencing (clean, gated, evidenced per stage) contrasts sharply with combat R2/R3 and decision_queue.md, which drifted out of sync with each other.
directions.diagonal: propagation-spec claims cross-scale reach but shows no linkage to mass-battle DG-3/DG-4 (landed same week, PR#75) or to threadwork.
directions.forwards: workplan v5 and roadmap_state.yaml, the two documents meant to orient forward sequencing, are both ~1 week stale on substance despite superficial recent touches.
directions.backwards: decision_queue.md's frozen items 1-3 still reflect pre-R2 combat physics, creating resumed-from-superseded risk for the next session that reads it in isolation.

findings.claim: The steering file that declares its own staleness a defect has not been updated with any of the last week's work (contest Stages 1a-3, R2 merge, R3, month-overview EDs, propagation-spec/doctrine ratification, mass-battle DG-3/4).
findings.cite: references/roadmap_state.yaml (updated: 2026-06-28; git log shows only a 07-01 path-repoint touch)

findings.claim: workplan v5 §3 still describes J-38 (propagation-spec authorship) as open/PROPOSED-to-come, while decision_queue.md item 18 and CURRENT.md mark it CANONICAL/resolved -- two steering docs give opposite answers for the same decision.
Items 1-3 (scene-combat Track-2 residuals) are frozen against pre-R2 physics and were never amended after R2 merged (07-04), risking a session acting on superseded context.
findings.cite: decision_queue.md items 1-3 vs HANDOFF_PC.md / CURRENT.md R2 merge entry

findings.claim: The month's flagship cross-scale-consequence architecture docs never mention threadwork or Coherence, despite P-14 and the Omega cross-scale clause.
R2 reduced spear/polearm dominance from 84-96% to 78-93% but the declared 55-75% contested band is still not met; R3 is the now-assigned remainder.
The degenerate ~87%/0%/0% seeded win-share remains unaddressed despite a month of otherwise-heavy CI/tooling investment.
Session-lane-scoping is documented but not CI-enforced, while contest-rebuild and combat R2/R3 already run as concurrent lane sessions unguarded.

subsystem: recent_infra (SEQUENCE lane: last-month infra/ecosystem work vs. the four steering surfaces)
